req.title: Audit log access (audit)
req.statement: A privacy-officer shall access audit logs scoped to their organisation; cross-organisation access shall be forbidden. An AuditEvent must be emitted for every operation and tested end-to-end.

doc.reasoning: The requirement mandates three distinct test concerns: (1) a privacy-officer can retrieve AuditEvent records scoped to their own organisation (positive/EP), (2) a privacy-officer is forbidden from accessing another organisation's audit logs (negative/NEG), and (3) a boundary case where the officer queries exactly at the org-scope boundary — i.e., a single AuditEvent record belonging to their org versus zero records when the filter yields nothing outside it (BVA). A fourth test verifies the end-to-end emission of an AuditEvent for the query operation itself (STC — the system must transition from 'operation performed' to 'audit record created'). A fifth test uses a Decision Table to cover the combined effect of role + org-scope on access.

tests.name: test_privacy_officer_can_read_own_org_audit_events
preconditions: User with role:privacy-officer exists for org_id=ORG_A
At least one AuditEvent record exists scoped to ORG_A
actions: Authenticate as privacy-officer of ORG_A to obtain bearer token
Assert HTTP 200 and parse response bundle
trace: R-HLT-089: privacy-officer shall access audit logs scoped to their organisation
tests.executable:
import pytest

def test_privacy_officer_can_read_own_org_audit_events(
    api_client,           # <fixture> returns authenticated httpx.Client
    officer_token_org_a,  # <fixture> bearer token for privacy-officer of ORG_A
    seed_audit_event_org_a,  # <fixture> ensures >=1 AuditEvent for ORG_A
    base_url,             # <fixture> e.g. "https://fhir-lite.internal"
    ORG_A,                # <fixture> org identifier string
):
    headers = {"Authorization": f"Bearer {officer_token_org_a}"}
    resp = api_client.get(
        f"{base_url}/AuditEvent",
        params={"organization": ORG_A},
        headers=headers,
    )
    assert resp.status_code == 200
    bundle = resp.json()
    assert bundle["resourceType"] == "Bundle"
    assert bundle.get("total", 0) >= 1
    for entry in bundle.get("entry", []):
        resource = entry["resource"]
        agents = resource.get("agent", [])
        org_refs = [
            a.get("who", {}).get("reference", "")
            for a in agents
        ]
        assert any(ORG_A in ref for ref in org_refs), (
            f"AuditEvent {resource.get('id')} not scoped to {ORG_A}"
        )

tests.name: test_privacy_officer_forbidden_from_cross_org_audit_events
preconditions: User with role:privacy-officer exists for org_id=ORG_A
At least one AuditEvent record exists scoped to ORG_B
actions: Authenticate as privacy-officer of ORG_A to obtain bearer token
Assert HTTP 403 and inspect error body
expected: Response body contains OperationOutcome or error code indicating forbidden
No AuditEvent data for ORG_B is disclosed
trace: R-HLT-089: cross-organisation access shall be forbidden
tests.executable:
import pytest

def test_privacy_officer_forbidden_from_cross_org_audit_events(
    api_client,
    officer_token_org_a,
    seed_audit_event_org_b,  # <fixture> ensures >=1 AuditEvent for ORG_B
    base_url,
    ORG_B,
):
    headers = {"Authorization": f"Bearer {officer_token_org_a}"}
    resp = api_client.get(
        f"{base_url}/AuditEvent",
        params={"organization": ORG_B},
        headers=headers,
    )
    assert resp.status_code == 403, (
        f"Expected 403 for cross-org access, got {resp.status_code}"
    )
    body = resp.json()
    # Must not leak any resource data
    assert "entry" not in body or body.get("entry") == []
    # Should return an OperationOutcome
    assert body.get("resourceType") == "OperationOutcome"

tests.name: test_audit_event_emitted_after_query_operation
preconditions: User with role:privacy-officer exists for org_id=ORG_A
System can emit AuditEvent upon successful /AuditEvent query
A known correlation_id can be injected via X-Request-ID header
actions: GET /AuditEvent?entity=<correlation_id> to retrieve the emitted audit record
Assert the system-emitted AuditEvent exists and references the operation
expected: A subsequent AuditEvent exists whose entity.what matches the correlation_id
trace: R-HLT-089: An AuditEvent must be emitted for every operation
tests.executable:
import uuid, time, pytest

def test_audit_event_emitted_after_query_operation(
    api_client,
    officer_token_org_a,
    base_url,
    ORG_A,
):
    correlation_id = str(uuid.uuid4())
    headers = {
        "Authorization": f"Bearer {officer_token_org_a}",
        "X-Request-ID": correlation_id,
    }
    resp = api_client.get(
        f"{base_url}/AuditEvent",
        params={"organization": ORG_A},
        headers=headers,
    )
    assert resp.status_code == 200

    # Allow async audit write to propagate
    time.sleep(1)

    audit_resp = api_client.get(
        f"{base_url}/AuditEvent",
        params={"entity": correlation_id},
        headers=headers,
    )
    assert audit_resp.status_code == 200
    bundle = audit_resp.json()
    assert bundle.get("total", 0) >= 1, "No AuditEvent emitted for operation"
    event = bundle["entry"][0]["resource"]
    assert event.get("action") == "R"
    assert str(event.get("outcome", "")) == "0"

tests.name: test_single_boundary_audit_event_within_org_scope
preconditions: User with role:privacy-officer exists for org_id=ORG_A
Exactly one AuditEvent record exists scoped to ORG_A (boundary minimum)
No additional records exist for ORG_A beyond that one
actions: Ensure exactly 1 AuditEvent exists for ORG_A using setup fixture
expected: Returned AuditEvent is scoped to ORG_A
trace: R-HLT-089: records returned within org (boundary: minimum one record)
R-HLT-089: privacy-officer shall access audit logs scoped to their organisation
tests.executable:
import pytest

def test_single_boundary_audit_event_within_org_scope(
    api_client,
    officer_token_org_a,
    ensure_exactly_one_audit_event_org_a,  # <fixture> idempotently seeds exactly 1 record
    base_url,
    ORG_A,
):
    headers = {"Authorization": f"Bearer {officer_token_org_a}"}
    resp = api_client.get(
        f"{base_url}/AuditEvent",
        params={"organization": ORG_A, "_count": "1"},
        headers=headers,
    )
    assert resp.status_code == 200
    bundle = resp.json()
    assert bundle["resourceType"] == "Bundle"
    assert bundle.get("total") == 1, (
        f"Expected exactly 1 AuditEvent, got {bundle.get('total')}"
    )
    entries = bundle.get("entry", [])
    assert len(entries) == 1
    resource = entries[0]["resource"]
    agents = resource.get("agent", [])
    org_refs = [a.get("who", {}).get("reference", "") for a in agents]
    assert any(ORG_A in ref for ref in org_refs)

tests.name: test_decision_table_role_and_org_combinations
actions: For each (role, org_param, expected_status) combination in decision table:
trace: R-HLT-089: cross-organisation access shall be forbidden
tests.executable:
import pytest

@pytest.mark.parametrize("token_fixture,org_param,expected_status", [
    ("officer_token_org_a",  "ORG_A", 200),
    ("officer_token_org_a",  "ORG_B", 403),
    ("user_token_org_a",     "ORG_A", 403),
    (None,                   "ORG_A", 401),
])
def test_decision_table_role_and_org_combinations(
    request,
    api_client,
    base_url,
    token_fixture,
    org_param,
    expected_status,
    ORG_A,
    ORG_B,
):
    token = request.getfixturevalue(token_fixture) if token_fixture else None
    headers = {"Authorization": f"Bearer {token}"} if token else {}
    resolved_org = ORG_A if org_param == "ORG_A" else ORG_B
    resp = api_client.get(
        f"{base_url}/AuditEvent",
        params={"organization": resolved_org},
        headers=headers,
    )
    assert resp.status_code == expected_status, (
        f"token={token_fixture} org={org_param}: "
        f"expected {expected_status}, got {resp.status_code}"
    )
